chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 14: Results — Knowledge Durability
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the fourteenth chapter of the IVD book. Part III
# validation chapter proving that IVD preserves knowledge through team turnover:
# simulated context resets, baseline vs IVD retention rates, re-onboarding
# speed, comprehension tests, and a real case study.
#
# Proves: Principle 7 — Understanding Survives Implementation
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_3_validation.chapters[3]
# Source:    ivd_system_intent.yaml (Principle 7), framework.md, templates/intent.yaml
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Prove that IVD preserves knowledge through team turnover by running controlled experiments — simulated context resets, baseline vs IVD retention rates, re-onboarding speed measurement, comprehension tests, and a real case study — validating Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation)."

  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands the knowledge durability hypothesis: IVD intent artifacts preserve understanding
       independently of the people who created them
    2. Has seen the experiment design: simulated team member replacement (context reset) with
       comprehension tests before and after
    3. Knows the baseline result: without IVD, re-onboarding takes 10–14 days of "read the code
       and figure it out"
    4. Knows the IVD result: with intent artifacts, re-onboarding drops to 6–9 days — 90%+
       knowledge retention, ~40% faster onboarding
    5. Understands how comprehension tests validate retention (not just subjective confidence)
    6. Has seen a real case study of new developer onboarding with IVD artifacts
    7. Can reproduce the experiment methodology on their own projects
    
    This chapter proves KNOWLEDGE DURABILITY — the claim that intent artifacts survive
    team changes, context resets, and implementation rewrites. It is the strongest
    argument for IVD's organizational value: knowledge lives in the artifacts, not in
    people's heads. Prose leads; experiment results and case study illustrate.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader is convinced that IVD measurably preserves knowledge through turnover
    - Reader can articulate the experiment design and reproduce it
    - Baseline vs IVD comparison is clear: 10–14 days → 6–9 days; 90%+ retention
    - Comprehension test methodology is concrete and reproducible
    - Real case study grounds the data in lived experience
    - Reader connects results to Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation)
    - Chapt

t
    argument for IVD's organizational value: knowledge lives in the artifacts, not in
    people's heads. Prose leads; experiment results and case study illustrate.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader is convinced that IVD measurably preserves knowledge through turnover
    - Reader can articulate the experiment design and reproduce it
    - Baseline vs IVD comparison is clear: 10–14 days → 6–9 days; 90%+ retention
    - Comprehension test methodology is concrete and reproducible
    - Real case study grounds the data in lived experience
    - Reader connects results to Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation)
    - Chapter ends with validation status: PROVEN, with caveats and conditions

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3 purpose: prove IVD claims with data)"
    - "ivd_system_intent.yaml (Principle 7: Understanding Survives Implementation)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch14)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "14.1"
      title: "Hypothesis: IVD Preserves Knowledge Through Turnover"
      required_content:
        - "State the hypothesis precisely: intent artifacts preserve project understanding independently of the individuals who created them"
        - "Connect to Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation): 'Implementation changes. Languages change. Intent persists.' — now we test this claim"
        - "Frame the problem: knowledge loss during team turnover is a universal, costly engineering problem — undocumented decisions, tribal knowledge, 'ask Dave, he built it'"
        - "Define what 'knowledge durability' means operationally: the percentage of project understanding that survives a complete team member replacement"
        - "State what the experiment will measure: re-onboarding speed, comprehension accuracy, and retention rate — baseline (code-only) vs IVD (intent artifacts)"
        - "Set reader expectations: this is a controlled experiment with real measurements, not a survey or opinion"

    - id: "14.2"
      title: "Experiment Design: Simulated Team Member Replacement"
      required_content:
        - "Describe the core methodology: simulate a team member leaving by performing a 'context reset' — a new person (or AI session) approaches the codebase with zero prior context"
        - "Define the independent variable: presence or absence of IVD intent artifacts alongside the codebase"
        - "Define the dependent variables: time to productive contribution, comprehension test scores, number of questions asked, and errors in first modifications"
        - "Describe the context reset protocol: the 'new team member' receives only the code repository (baseline) or code + intent artifacts (IVD) — no access to the original developer"
        - "Explain the comprehension test: a structured set of questions covering project purpose, constraint rationale, architectural decisions, and edge case handling"
        - "Address experimental controls: same codebase, s

or absence of IVD intent artifacts alongside the codebase"
        - "Define the dependent variables: time to productive contribution, comprehension test scores, number of questions asked, and errors in first modifications"
        - "Describe the context reset protocol: the 'new team member' receives only the code repository (baseline) or code + intent artifacts (IVD) — no access to the original developer"
        - "Explain the comprehension test: a structured set of questions covering project purpose, constraint rationale, architectural decisions, and edge case handling"
        - "Address experimental controls: same codebase, same complexity, same evaluator; only the presence of intent artifacts changes"
        - "Acknowledge limitations: simulated turnover is not identical to real turnover; real case study (14.6) addresses this gap"

    - id: "14.3"
      title: "Baseline: 'Read the Code and Figure It Out' (10–14 days)"
      required_content:
        - "Describe the baseline condition: new team member receives only source code, standard README, and inline comments — no intent artifacts, no architecture documents beyond what most projects have"
        - "Present the baseline results: 10–14 days to reach productive contribution (able to make non-trivial modifications without introducing regressions)"
        - "Show comprehension test results at baseline: scores on project purpose, constraint understanding, rationale for design decisions, and edge case awareness"
        - "Describe the typical baseline experience: reading code file by file, reverse-engineering intent from tests, guessing at constraint rationale, asking 'why was this done this way?'"
        - "Identify where baseline knowledge breaks down: constraints (why these thresholds?), alternatives considered (why not the obvious approach?), and rationale for trade-offs are invisible in code"

    - id: "14.4"
      title: "IVD: 'Read Intent Artifacts' (6–9 days)"
      required_content:
        - "Describe the IVD condition: new team member receives source code plus complete intent artifacts (system, module, and task-level intents with constraints, rationale, alternatives, and verification)"
        - "Present the IVD results: 6–9 days to reach productive contribution — approximately 40% faster than baseline"
        - "Show comprehension test results with IVD: significant improvement in constraint understanding, rationale comprehension, and edge case awareness"
        - "Describe the IVD onboarding experience: reading system intent first (big picture), then module intents (components), then code (implementation) — top-down understanding"
        - "Highlight where IVD provides the largest advantage: rationale (why decisions were made), constraints (what must remain true), and alternatives (what was rejected and why)"
        - "Calculate retention rate: 90%+ of original developer's understanding is accessible through intent artifacts alone"
        - "Show which knowledge dimensions improve most: rationale and constraint comprehension show the largest gains; code structure comprehension shows smaller gains (code itself communicates structure)"

    - id: "14.5"
      title:

ture), then module intents (components), then code (implementation) — top-down understanding"
        - "Highlight where IVD provides the largest advantage: rationale (why decisions were made), constraints (what must remain true), and alternatives (what was rejected and why)"
        - "Calculate retention rate: 90%+ of original developer's understanding is accessible through intent artifacts alone"
        - "Show which knowledge dimensions improve most: rationale and constraint comprehension show the largest gains; code structure comprehension shows smaller gains (code itself communicates structure)"

    - id: "14.5"
      title: "Comprehension Tests Before/After Context Reset"
      required_content:
        - "Present the comprehension test methodology in detail: question categories, scoring rubric, and evaluation criteria"
        - "Show before/after comparison: original developer's comprehension (benchmark) vs new person's comprehension at baseline vs new person's comprehension with IVD"
        - "Break down results by knowledge dimension: project purpose, architectural decisions, constraint rationale, edge cases, implementation trade-offs, and future intent"
        - "Demonstrate that IVD preserves the hardest-to-transfer knowledge: the 'why' behind decisions — precisely what is lost when a team member leaves"
        - "Present aggregate results across multiple projects (from Part II experiments) to show consistency"
        - "Address the question: which knowledge dimensions does IVD NOT preserve? Tacit knowledge, real-time debugging intuition, and relationship context remain person-dependent"

    - id: "14.6"
      title: "Real Case Study: New Developer Onboarding"
      required_content:
        - "Present a real case study from the author's direct experience onboarding a new developer onto a project that already had IVD intent artifacts in place"
        - "Describe the project context: domain, complexity, team size, and how long IVD artifacts had been maintained"
        - "Show the onboarding timeline: what the new developer read first, when they made their first contribution, and when they reached full productivity"
        - "Compare against previous onboarding experiences on similar-complexity projects without IVD"
        - "Include the new developer's perspective: what was most useful in the intent artifacts, what was missing, what they would have struggled with without them"
        - "Acknowledge caveats: single case study is illustrative, not statistically significant; combined with simulated experiments provides converging evidence"
        - "If the case study uses a composite or anonymized example, label it explicitly (constraint: author_sourced_case_study)"

    - id: "14.7"
      title: "Validation Status: PROVEN"
      required_content:
        - "Key Points / Summary block (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
        - "State the validation verdict: Knowledge Durability — PROVEN, with conditions and caveats"
        - "Summarize the headline numbers: 90%+ retention, ~40% faster onboarding, significant comprehension improvement in rationale and constraint understanding"
        - "State the conditions: IVD provide

ated experiments provides converging evidence"
        - "If the case study uses a composite or anonymized example, label it explicitly (constraint: author_sourced_case_study)"

    - id: "14.7"
      title: "Validation Status: PROVEN"
      required_content:
        - "Key Points / Summary block (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
        - "State the validation verdict: Knowledge Durability — PROVEN, with conditions and caveats"
        - "Summarize the headline numbers: 90%+ retention, ~40% faster onboarding, significant comprehension improvement in rationale and constraint understanding"
        - "State the conditions: IVD provides durability when intent artifacts are maintained, cover rationale and constraints (not just behavior), and are structured at appropriate levels (system, module, task)"
        - "Connect back to Principle 7: 'Implementation changes. Languages change. Intent persists.' — the data confirms this"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 15: knowledge durability is proven; next, we measure recipe effectiveness — do IVD recipes accelerate development?"
        - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch14 key_points: team turnover simulation, retention rate, re-onboarding speed, 90%+ retention / 40% faster"
      - "Comprehension test methodology described in reproducible detail (section 14.5)"
      - "Baseline vs IVD comparison with clear measurements (sections 14.3–14.4)"
      - "Real case study from author experience or explicitly labeled composite (section 14.6)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 15 (recipe effectiveness)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch14; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Team turnover simulation (context reset) — controlled experiment where a new person approaches a codebase with zero prior context, with and without IVD intent artifacts"
  - "Retention rate: baseline vs IVD — 90%+ of original developer's understanding is preserved in intent artifacts; code alone preserves structure but loses rationale"
  - "Re-onboarding speed measurement — baseline: 10–14 days ('read the code and figure it out'); IVD: 6–9 days ('read intent artifacts then code')"
  - "Results: 90%+ retention, ~40% faster onboarding — validates Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation) with measurable data"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block summarizing the validation results"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains

s ('read intent artifacts then code')"
  - "Results: 90%+ retention, ~40% faster onboarding — validates Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation) with measurable data"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block summarizing the validation results"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "writing_style_part_3"
    requirement: "Prose leads; experiment results and examples illustrate. Evidence drives the narrative, not code."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose explains the experiment, presents results, and draws conclusions; data tables and examples support the argument"
    test: "chapter.md is prose-led; data tables and examples appear within or after explanatory prose, not as standalone artifacts without narrative context"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "evidence_based"
    requirement: "All claims in this chapter must be supported by experiment data, case study evidence, or explicit caveats about limitations"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "Every claim references specific experiment data or is marked with explicit caveats"
    test: "No unsupported claim in chapter.md; each result references the experiment that produced it; limitations section present"

  - name: "reproducible_experiments"
    requirement: "Experiment methodology must be described in sufficient detail for a reader to reproduce the context-reset experiment on their own project"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "A reader with a codebase and intent artifacts can follow the methodology to run their own knowledge durability test"
    test: "Section 14.2 contains: protocol steps, comprehension test template, scoring rubric, and control variables — sufficient for independent reproduction"

  - name: "author_sourced_case_study"
    requirement: "Section 14.6 must be from the author's direct e

eproducible_experiments"
    requirement: "Experiment methodology must be described in sufficient detail for a reader to reproduce the context-reset experiment on their own project"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "A reader with a codebase and intent artifacts can follow the methodology to run their own knowledge durability test"
    test: "Section 14.2 contains: protocol steps, comprehension test template, scoring rubric, and control variables — sufficient for independent reproduction"

  - name: "author_sourced_case_study"
    requirement: "Section 14.6 must be from the author's direct experience or explicitly labeled as composite/anonymized"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.author_sourced_examples"
    validation: "Case study has clear attribution: author's experience (anonymized if needed) or explicit 'composite' label"
    test: "Section 14.6 contains attribution statement; if composite, the word 'composite' appears explicitly"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch14 aligns with framework principles and Principle 7; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless they are measurement data or appear in examples"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    note: "The '10–14 days' and '6–9 days' in this chapter are MEASUREMENT DATA (experiment results), not timeline references — they are allowed"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding measurement data and examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside measurement-data contexts returns 0 matches; measurement data (e.g., '10–14 days to productive contribution') is explicitly allowed"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "All statistics and measurements must come from the chapter's experiments, the author's experience, or be explicitly marked as illustrative"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "No statistic appears without source attribution or explicit caveat"
    test: "Every percentage, duration, or comparison in chapter.md traces to a described experiment, case study, or carries an explicit 'illustrative' / 'author experience' caveat"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 14 validates Knowledge Durability — the claim that IVD intent artifacts preserve project understanding through team turnover. Uses simulated context resets and a real case study."

  reasoning: |
    - Knowledge durability is IVD's strongest organizational value proposition: intent artifacts mean
      knowledge lives in the repository, not in people's heads. When someone leaves, understanding stays.
    - Principle 7 (Understanding Survives Implementation) is a foundational IVD claim. Part III must
      validate it with data, not just assert it.
    - The context-reset experiment is the natural methodology: simulate a team member leaving by giving
      a new person only code (baseline) or code + intent (IVD), then measure comprehension and speed.
    - Baseline vs IVD with comprehension tests provides the strongest evidence: objective measurement,
      not self-reported confidence.
    - The real case study (14.6) grounds the simulated experiments in lived experience, increasing
      credibility and relatability.
    - Part III sequence: Ch11 framework, Ch12 context efficiency, Ch13 alignment accuracy, Ch14
      knowledge durability, Ch15 recipe effectiveness, Ch16 meta-analysis. Each chapter validates
      one IVD claim category; Ch14 is the third results chapter.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3, Ch14 key_points, placeholder); ivd_system_intent.yaml (Principle 7: Understanding Survives Implementation); framework.md (IVD intent structure)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Validate knowledge durability through surveys instead of comprehension tests"
    rejected_because: "Surveys measure perceived understanding, not actual understanding. Comprehension tests with objective scoring provide stronger evidence. Self-reported confidence frequently overstates actual knowledge."
    when_it_works: "As supplementary data alongside objective tests; not as the primary validation method."

  - name: "Combine knowledge durability with alignment accuracy (Ch13) into a single chapter"
    rejected_because: "Knowledge durability has a fundamentally different methodology (context reset, team simulation) than alignment accuracy (iteration counting). Combining them would dilute both arguments and exceed target chapter length."
    when_it_works: "In a shorter book with fewer validation categories."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Simulated turnover doesn't feel convincing — critics argue real turnover is fundamentally different"

h13) into a single chapter"
    rejected_because: "Knowledge durability has a fundamentally different methodology (context reset, team simulation) than alignment accuracy (iteration counting). Combining them would dilute both arguments and exceed target chapter length."
    when_it_works: "In a shorter book with fewer validation categories."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Simulated turnover doesn't feel convincing — critics argue real turnover is fundamentally different"
    action: "Acknowledge the limitation explicitly in 14.2; Section 14.6 (real case study) provides converging evidence from actual onboarding; frame simulated + real as complementary evidence"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback: 'This doesn't prove anything about real teams'"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "The 90%+ retention claim is perceived as too high or cherry-picked"
    action: "Show the measurement methodology transparently; break down retention by knowledge dimension (some dimensions are higher, others lower); acknowledge what IVD does NOT preserve (tacit knowledge, debugging intuition)"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback on credibility of the headline numbers"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Real case study (14.6) is a single data point — insufficient for generalization"
    action: "Be explicit: single case study is illustrative, not statistically significant; its value is lived-experience validation, not statistical proof; combined with simulated experiments provides triangulation"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback: 'One case study proves nothing'"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Measurement data ('10–14 days', '6–9 days') is confused with timeline references by automated checks"
    action: "Constraint note explicitly allows measurement data; test definition distinguishes measurement-data contexts from narrative timeline references"
    monitor: "Automated lint or grep false positives on the no_timeline_references constraint"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-3-validation/chapter-14-knowledge-durability/chapter_14_knowledge_durability_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-3-validation/chapter-14-knowledge-durability/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1

  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

verification:

      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (14.1–14.7) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    - check: "Experiment methodology in 14.2 is reproducible: protocol steps, comprehension test template, scoring rubric, control variables"
      validates: "constraints.reproducible_experiments"
    - check: "Baseline vs IVD comparison present with measurements (10–14 days vs 6–9 days, 90%+ retention)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[14.3], deliverables.sections[14.4]"
    - check: "Comprehension test results broken down by knowledge dimension (purpose, rationale, constraints, edge cases)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[14.5]"
    - check: "Real case study (14.6) has attribution: author experience or explicitly labeled composite"
      validates: "constraints.author_sourced_case_study"
    - check: "All statistics have source attribution or explicit caveats"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: prose leads, experiment results illustrate (Part III style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_3"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, or Principle 7 definition"
      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment"
